issue_title: Boolean inputs lower-cased before being compared to true_values and false_values
issue_body:
When the parameters `true_values` and `false_values` are set for a boolean inputs are to that field are checked against values in those lists, however those input values are being lower cased (see the code [here](https://github.com/Pylons/colander/blob/1.3.2/colander/__init__.py#L1458)) and the true and false values are not, leading this code to:

```py
node.typ = colander.Boolean(
    false_choices=('false', 'N', '0'),
    true_choices=('true', 'Y')
)
```
to produce this error:
> "Y" is neither in ('false', 'N', '0') nor in ('true', 'Y')

I would suggest that either the lower casing of the boolean inputs should be removed, or if these settings are intended to be case insensitive lowercase the true and false list values and add a note to the docs.

issue_title: Implement new submodule handling
issue_body:
In previous git versions, it was common to checkout submodules right at the spot in the tree of their parent repository.

In later git versions though, submodule repositories are placed outside of the working tree.

Git-python should choose the submodule mode depending on the python version, and thus remain backwards compatible.

issue_title: upload_from_path does not close the file
issue_body:
as can be seen from the following snippet:
https://github.com/Imgur/imgurpython/blob/534c2c8d346e7067b78036f7ece9a1d36a6be18e/imgurpython/client.py#L582-L596

The file needs to be closed after being opened. The code above can be easily changed into the following:

```
with open(path, 'rb') as fd:
    contents = fd.read()   
b64 = base64.b64encode(contents)
``` 

And the issue will be fixed. The issue is that a BufferedReader is left open and never closed.

issue_title: Externally useful attributes should be declared public and not start with an underscore
issue_body:
Old Selenium2Library has several attributes that are useful externally from custom libraries and extensions:

```
_run_on_failure_keyword
_speed_in_secs
_timeout_in_secs
_implicit_wait_in_secs
_current_browser()
```

We should indicate that using these externally is OK by removing the leading underscore. The current browser should also be accessible as a normal attribute (i.e. without calling a method) and I feel `browser` is better than `current_browser`. I would also remove unnecessary `_in_secs` postfixes. In other words, I would like to have these attributes instead:

```
run_on_failure_keyword
speed
timeout
implicit_wait
browser
```

Renaming these attributes is obviously a backwards incompatible change. We should thus add propertys that enable the old functionality but emit deprecation warnings. These propertys can then be removed in 3.1 or later.

issue_title: [Feature request] update packages _only_ if pipefile.lock is missing or out of date
issue_body:
We distribute a script which in turn uses pipenv to run some python code. We do this to be able to have our script be portable.

What we are looking for is a way to check/verify that the machine the script is running on has the latest version of the pipfile.lock file.

Workaround is to always run `pipenv install` which works the way we want IF the pipefile.lock is missing or invalid. But it doesn't when everything is OK. Meaning, what happens then is that it uninstalls/removes everything and reinstalls. The option, if present, would simply do nothing at this point.

I hope that makes sense?

issue_title: Goal: ensure lock file consistency after all installation commands
issue_body:
As noted in https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/1137, `pipenv` doesn't currently check for requirement conflicts between `[packages]` and `[dev-packages]`, which can cause problems when attempting to generate a flat `requirements.txt` that covers both, as well as causing general weirdness when dependencies conflict.

The way `pipenv install` works (running the installation and then updating the lock file) means it is also relatively easy for the local environment to get out of sync with the lock file: if the installation request covers an already installed package, then it won't be updated locally, but the lock file will be updated to the latest version available from any configured package indices.

This issue doesn't cover an individual feature request. Instead, it establishes the goal of helping to ensure consistency between the lock file and the local environment by structuring installation commands to modify the lock file *first*, and then use the updated lock file to drive the actual installation step. (We don't have any kind of target ETA for how long this work will take - writing it down is just intended to help ensure we're all heading in the same direction, and are all comfortable with that direction)

A key aspect of this is going to be clarifying the division of responsibilities between `pipenv install`, `pipenv uninstall`, `pipenv lock`, and `pipenv update` (and potentially adding one or more new subcommands if deemed necessary).

Proposed substeps:

- [x] add a `pipenv sync` subcommand that ensures that the current environment matches the lock file (akin to `pip-sync` in `pip-tools`). (Implemented by @kennethreitz for 10.0.0, with `pipenv sync` ensuring versions of locked packages match the lock file, while `pipenv clean` removes packages that aren't in the lock file. I like that change relative to `pip-sync`, as it means that including an implicit sync in another command won't unexpectedly delete anything from the virtual environment)
- [x] Keep the current behaviour for `pipenv install` with no arguments, but switch to recommending the use of `pipenv sync` when setting up a fresh environment (that way `pipenv install` is used mainly for *changing* the installed components)
- [x] update `pipenv lock` to always ensure that `[packages]` and `[dev-packages]` are consistent with each other (resolution for #1137)
- [x] change `pipenv update` to be equivalent to `pipenv lock && pipenv sync` (pipenv update is instead being removed entirely - it's old behaviour was actually comparable to what is now `pipenv sync && pipenv clean`)
- [x] add a new `pipenv lock --keep-outdated` option that still generates a fresh `Pipfile.lock` from `Pipfile`, but minimises the changes made to only those needed to satisfy any changes made to `Pipfile` (whether that's package additions, removals, or changes to version constraints)
- [x] change `pipenv install <packages>` to be equivalent to "add or update entries in Pipfile" followed by `pipenv lock && pipenv sync` (implemented in #1486).
- [x] Add a `--keep-outdated` option to pipenv install that it passes through to the `pipenv lock` operation.
- [x] add a new `pipenv install --selective-upgrade <packages>` feature that's semantically equivalent to "remove those package entries from Pipfile (if present)", run `pipenv lock --keep-outdated`, then run `pipenv install --keep-outdated <packages>` (this is the final step that delivers support for https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/966). If just a package name is given, then the existing version constraint in `Pipfile` is used, otherwise the given version constraint overwrites the existing one. The effect of this on the current environment should be the same as `pip install --upgrade --upgrade-strategy=only-if-needed <packages>` in pip 9+ (except that `Pipfile.lock` will also be updated).

If anyone wants to pick up one of these substeps, make a comment below to say you're working on it (to attempt to minimise duplication of effort), then file a PR linking back to this issue. (Steps listed earlier will need to be completed before later steps are practical)

Open questions:

- None

Resolved questions (at least for the immediate future):

- `pipenv lock` will continue to default to upgrading everything by default, with `pipenv lock --keep-outdated` to request a minimal update that only adjusts the lock file to account for `Pipfile` changes (additions, removals, and changes to version constraints)
- `pipenv lock --skip-lock` will continue to work as it does today (even though it means the lock file and the local environment can get out of sync:  use `pipenv sync && pipenv clean` to resync them)
- `pipenv install` and `pipenv install <package>` will continue to imply a full `pipenv lock` by default, with `pipenv install --keep-outdated` needed to request only the minimal changes required to satisfy the installation request
- `pipenv install <package>` will continue to retain the existing version constraint in `Pipfile` if none is given on the command line, even for the new `--selective-upgrade` option
- `pipenv uninstall <package>` will just remove the specified package[s], and hence may leave no longer needed dependencies in the local environment. Running `pipenv lock && pipenv sync && pipenv clean` will clear them out.

----

Note: the original proposal here was just to ensure that `[dev-packages]` and `[packages]` were kept in sync when generating the lock file, and the first few posts reflect that. The current proposal instead covers the lock file driven symmetric update proposal first mentioned in https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/1255#issuecomment-354585775

issue_title: Streaming pricing performance
issue_body:
I would strongly suggest changing `chunk_size` from 1 byte to a typical size of a Price packet. This will introduce a very little delay in packet processing but at the same time it will significantly decrease CPU usage.

The line that needs to be changed is [__init__.py#L186](https://github.com/oanda/v20-python/blob/master/src/v20/__init__.py#L186)

Subscribe to all instruments and you'll see how hard is the performance hit with `iter_lines(1)`

issue_title: Issue with README
issue_body:
README describes that enums should be declared inside the class they are used in. 
```python
class MyModel(models.Model):

    class Color(Enum):
        RED = 'r'
        GREEN = 'g'
        BLUE = 'b'

    color = EnumField(Color, max_length=1)
```

This causes problems with Django because migrations cannot import the missing `Color` class. 

I think the proper example given should be something in the lines of: 
```python
class Color(Enum):
    RED = 'r'
    GREEN = 'g'
    BLUE = 'b'

class MyModel(models.Model):
    color = EnumField(Color, max_length=1)
```

issue_title: producer should be resilient to broker failure
issue_body: When a broker in a multi-node cluster with replicated topics goes down, the producer should not crash. Instead, it should continue producing messages to all available brokers. The producer should also be able to start when at least `<replication factor>` brokers in the cluster are up.

issue_title: Support `chunk` option to enable "at-least-once" delivery
issue_body:
### Background

According to the "Forward Protocol Specification v1", fluentd supports an option named `chunk` which enables at-least-once delivery of messages.

This option is very useful in cases where data loss is not acceptable.

> https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/wiki/Forward-Protocol-Specification-v1#option

### The problem

The current design of fluent-logger-python, however, makes it difficult to support this new option. 
Specifically:

1. Events are buffered inside `FluentSender` class as a single `bytes` sequence (`self.pendings`). There is no efficient way to reconstruct a specific event from the buffer and resend it.
2. And this `bytes` sequence buffer is kinda API. So we cannot moddify the format in which `FluentSender` buffers messages (at least, casually) or it will break many user-defined `buffer_overflow_handler`s.
3. Also for now, we lack a handful of building blocks for supporting the "at-least-once" semantics. For example, there is no reliable mechanism for users to tell if a message has been delivered successfully [^]

### So we need to ...

The bottom line is, we need to apply some architectural changes to make this library support the (newly-introduced) "at-least-once" semantics. Of course, we need to do it without breaking many existing programs.

What do you think about this? Or is there already a plan to make this library compliant with the v1 specification?

----

[^] Yes, `FluentSender.emit()` is supposed to notify this via its return value. But even if the method returns False, the message might be delivered anyway through the pending buffer, and this "retry" part is totally opaque to users.

issue_title: Performance regression in morphology.watershed from 0.12.3 to 0.13.0
issue_body:
## Description
Great performance degradation in skimage.morphology.watershed for ver. 0.13.0 in comparison with ver. 0.12.3.
3D images with size about (500, 500, 500) were testes.

## Way to reproduce
```
import numpy as np
from scipy import ndimage
from skimage import feature
from skimage import morphology
import time

dimensions = 500  
z, y, x = np.indices((dimensions, dimensions + 5, dimensions + 10))
radius = 100
xc, yc, zc = dimensions // 3 - 6, dimensions // 3, dimensions // 3 + 6
test_image = np.int32((x - xc) ** 2 + (y - yc) ** 2 + (z - zc) ** 2 <= radius ** 2)
test_image[zc - 5, yc - 5, xc - 5] = 0
test_image[zc - 5, yc + 5, xc - 5] = 0
test_image[zc - 5, yc + 4, xc - 5] = 0
xc *= 2
yc *= 2
zc *= 2
test_image[zc:zc + 100, yc:yc + 100, xc:xc + 100] = 2
test_image[zc + 10 + 3, yc + 10 + 3, xc + 10 - 3] = 0
test_image[zc + 10 - 3, yc + 10 - 3, xc + 10 + 3] = 0
test_image[zc + 10 + 3, yc + 10 - 3, xc + 10 + 3] = 0
test_image[zc-5, yc-5, xc-5] = 3

dist = ndimage.distance_transform_edt(test_image)
local_maxi = feature.peak_local_max(dist, min_distance=2, indices=False)
labels, num_obj = ndimage.label(local_maxi)
start_t = time.clock()
labels = morphology.watershed(-dist, labels, connectivity=ndimage.generate_binary_structure(3, 3), mask=test_image)
print(time.clock() - start_t)
```

Processing time for ver. 0.12.3 is about 16 s on my computer.
Processing time for ver. 0.13.0 is about 90 s. 
Slowing down about 4 times!!! 

Python version 3.5 for Windows
scikit-image versions 0.12.3 and 0.13.0

issue_title: Uninitialized timer variable in Region.cpp
issue_body:
Region level timers in the Network API provide valuable profiling information. 

In Region.cpp/hpp there is a boolean variable called `profilingEnabled_` which controls whether region timers are enabled for each region.  This variable is not initialized in the constructor, which means it is randomly initialized.

It should be initialized to false in the constructor.

We should plug the two together !

issue_title: RFC commit message style and release notes
issue_body:
### About

This RFC summarises reasons behind [git commit log message style](http://invenio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/technology/git.html#r2-remarks-on-commit-log-messages) that
was born in the Invenio 1 (and 2) monolithic world. Most of the original reasons
do not apply in the Invenio 3 independent package world anymore. Now that we are
approaching the first Invenio 3 stable release, the time is perfect to reflect
on the existing practices and how to improve them.

**Note:** This will be the topic of an [Invenio Developer Forum](https://indico.cern.ch/category/6046/) 
live discussions, so even though every one is encouraged to comment in here, it would
be great to meet up and reflect on the matter IRL together.

### 1. General upstream conventions

Upstream git conventions talk about a commit log headline, followed by a blank
line, followed by the multi-line body. Just as we use PEP-8 for Python code, we
use these upstream general git conventions for commit log messages. (Users of
Emacs, Vim and possibly other code editors have a "commit log mode" that helps
to format the messages according to these general conventions.) This RFC is not
questioning these; it focuses solely on the Invenio-specific semantics expressed
inside.

### 2. Commit headline format

In Invenio 1 (and 2), the convention was to use:

    component: brief description

permitting to rapidly distinguish commits to various modules:

    BibFormat: initial release of mobile app formats
    WebSearch: display number of hits in mobile output
    BibDocFile: improve BibDoc display in Files tab

In Invenio 3, the modules have been split into independent packages, making the
original intent not applicable.

Do we consider the separate leading component as still useful? If so, do we use
it to denote component of a package (docs, test, ui, core, api, etc) or nature
of the commit (bug, feature, enhancement, etc) or something else? If not, do we
prefer the headline to be a simple free style phrase? Do we pay attention to
headlines while bisecting?

### 3. Commit signatures

In Invenio 1 (and 2), we have been using Trac and Jenkins, the commit signatures
expressed the commit kwalitee and review status:

    Signed-off-by: Tibor Simko <tibor.simko@cern.ch>
    Reviewed-by: Tibor Simko <tibor.simko@cern.ch>
    Reported-by: Tibor Simko <tibor.simko@cern.ch>
    Co-authored-by: Tibor Simko <tibor.simko@cern.ch>

In Invenio 3, we are using GitHub where centralised reviews happen, and Travis
where commit quality and CI tests are enforced.

Do we keep some of the signatures to express credit for reporting and/or
co-authoring? Do we remove all signatures?

### 4. Release notes vs commit notes

In Invenio 1 (and 2), the point releases were rare, and services such as INSPIRE
often used ``master`` branch in production, and wanted to know what changed
precisely between any two given ``SHA1a`` and ``SHA1b``. Hence the release notes
that were compiled "live" for any commit log message range:

    kwalitee prepare release -c sha1a..sha1b

In Invenio 3, the point releases of various packages happen very frequently and
services are often deploying from PyPI releases only. (Side note: pinning
specific versions that are "waiting" for release due to some blockers.)

Do we consider it useful to keep relying on git log based messages to compile
release notes? Do we consider it sufficient to maintain a separate RELEASE-NOTES
file dissociated from commit logs? Do we consider it useful to be able to
compile changes automatically at any point in time? Do we compile them only at
the release time? Do we pay attention to release notes vs commit notes while
bisecting?

### 5. Release note classification

In Invenio 1, the release note messages were originally compiled from short
commit log headlines, see for example [1.1.0 release notes](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/releases/tag/v1.1.0).
This was often too brief to be understandable by the users.

This led to the introduction of commit log labels such as NEW, BETTER, FIX in
front of longer sentences, so that the release notes would be much more
self-understood and and categorised. See for example [1.2.1 release notes](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/releases/tag/v1.2.1).

Do we want to keep or amend the NEW/FIX/BETTER/SECURITY/etc release note
classification?

### 6. Release note author

In Invenio 1 (and 2), the release notes were created from the commit log
messages via commands like:

     kwalitee prepare release -c v1.1.6..

Hence it was important to use proper English phrases in the commit log messages.
Oftentimes developers didn't pay attention to this, which necessitated (i)
cherry-picking by the maintainer and/or (ii) rebasing by the developer.

Do we want the release note messages to be written by the contributors or by the
maintainers? Do we let maintainers rewrite the release notes either by
cherry-picking, or by using explicit merge commits, or by maintaining a separate
file, or by amending GitHub issue body, etc? Is the PR author the best person to
think of the release message? Is it the maintainer?

### 7. Body format

In Invenio 1 (and 2), the convention was to use itemised list:

    * BETTER Improves foo.

    * FIX Fixes bar.

permitting to express different facets of what the given commit does. (For
example, enhances a feature and fixes a related bug at the same time.)

Do we consider this still useful? Do we require one commit to announce one thing
and one thing only?

### 8. Issue integrations

In Invenio 1 (and 2), the link between commits, PRs, and issues were expressed
via usual directives:

     (addresses #1234)
     (closes #1234)

The format was parenthesised so that release notes could be properly compiled.
Some developers didn't use this format, requiring usual amendment cycle. Are we
happy with the practices? Are we remove the constraints?

### 9. Message format checks

If we retain using some structured commit message format, it should be easy to 
check and plug into Travis CI. Currently, we use:

    kwalitee check message master..upstream/pr/13

and this is plugged into CI for several repositories. Do developers run these locally? 
Do we plug these into Travis CI everywhere?

### Proposal time!

The above points summarised the main reasons behind current practices. Many of
the reasons are no longer valid in the world of Invenio 3 distributed packages.
Now that we are about to release the first Invenio 3 "production" packages,
the time is good to ponder and improve the system.

The high-level goals for selecting a consistent commit note system for Invenio
packages:

- the commits should be atomic etc (as usual);
- the commit message should describe changes fully, so that it would be
  self-understood what the commit does, even without reading the code;
- the commit notes should facilitate later bisecting;
- the release notes should be easily maintainable and not prone to getting 
  forgotten or getting out of sync with the code;
- the release notes should be informative to the users.

Let's discuss any finer points and any proposals live at an [Invenio Developer Forum](https://indico.cern.ch/category/6046/).

CC @inveniosoftware/developers

issue_title: WebSearch: SPIRES syntax converter parenthesis bug
issue_body:
In inspirehep/invenio#71 @tsgit reports:
[...]
these 2 searches
`find collaboration LIGO and a whiting, b f and a Weiss, r`
`find collaboration LIGO and (a whiting, b f and a Weiss, r)`
return vastly different results (90 vs 152 records)

this is because 
`find collaboration LIGO and (a whiting, b f and a Weiss, r)`
is incorrectly converted to
`collaboration:LIGO and ((author:"whiting, b* f*") and (author:"Weiss, r)*" or exactauthor:"Weiss, r *" or exactauthor:"Weiss, r" or author:"Weiss, r), *")`

where you see the closing paren becomes part of the author search term. Invenio search then ignores all parens, since they are mismatched, the warning message is

`"Search syntax misunderstood. Ignoring all parentheses in the query. If this doesn't help, please check your search and try again."`

so now we have a *boolean or* search for `Weiss, r` which finds additional records. The underlying problem is *spires-to-inveno* syntax conversion's handling of the parenthesized expression.
[...]

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_labels: [{'id': 414883316, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/labels/Size:%20easy', 'name': 'Size: easy', 'color': 'bc3cbc', 'default': False}, {'id': 414883285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/labels/Status:%20ready%20for%20work', 'name': 'Status: ready for work', 'color': '149414', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: Versioning of Components
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.component/+bug/97916, @srichter reported:

> I found the following comment from djay in the Wikis, which we should really consider:
>
> One thing I'd like to see is support for version numbering of components. This is to support the evolution of components. Content components for instance may have more attibutes added at some point. It would be nice if all instances did not just up and change themselves but you had control over the migration. esp if you could have special code that did the specific transformations for you (eg the recent Catalog data structure change).
>
> The same need for control of change applies to interfaces. Interfaces will change and unless your components are tied to specific versions of those interfaces you will not be able to guarentee anything thus defeating their purpose. You can argue that you should change the name of an interface everytime it changes but I think this could be very ugly very quickly.

issue_title: PersistentAdapterRegistry not compatible with pickle protocol 2
issue_body:
This was first discussed in unreleated comments in zopefoundation/Zope#188. Putting it here where it belongs:

There's this nice warning message on the pickle protocol page (https://docs.python.org/2/library/pickle.html):

> Note: For new-style classes, if getstate() returns a false value, the `__setstate__` method will not be called.

The code in https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.component/blob/master/src/zope/component/persistentregistry.py doesn't work with that for empty registries.

In the `__getstate__` it removes all its in-memory-only bits (everything in `_delegated`), but that means it returns an empty value for an empty registry. When unpickling things, `__init__` isn't called, so this code relies on the `self._createLookup()` to be called as part of `__setstate__` to put those in-memory objects back. Only this no longer happens thanks to the empty `__getstate__` return value. I think that PersistentAdapterRegistry needs to implement a `__new__` to populate its invariants, maybe a `__getnewargs__` as well.

issue_title: Race condition in case of near simultaneous POST API calls
issue_body:
Some of Djoser views are vulnerable to a race condition (which is not really a security issue unless you've setup debug in production). Basically saying, the problem is that in case of near simultaneous requests to endpoint with a POST method, which creates object for a model with a field with unique constraint, it will result in an `IntegrityError` and will return 500 to the client. 

It happens because of mentioned race condition, where validation step passes for both requests and than it fails on transaction to database.

issue_title: Carousel: add_widget after build()
issue_body:
The below example should be small enough to illustrate this issue. Basically, when a widget (slide) is added to a Carousel widget _after_ the build() function, i.e. using a Clock.schedule_once call, the widget is not properly sized to fit its container. Below this is illustrated using an AsyncImage widget. The first two slides are properly scaled, but after the allotted ten seconds have expired, the third slide is added but is _very_ small and shrinks into the bottom-left corner of its container.

This same example works without this issue when you run the same code on a desktop (here, specifically, Ubuntu 12.10); the third slide is properly added and resized. **I'm using the Kivy Launcher app on Android to run this code on an Android 4.0.3 device, which is where I'm seeing this issue.**

Thank you for your attention and time.

```
from kivy.app import App
from kivy.uix.carousel import Carousel
from kivy.factory import Factory
from kivy.clock import Clock
class Example1(App):

    def build(self):
        for i in range(2):
            src = "http://placehold.it/480x270.png&text=slide-%d&.png" % i
            image = Factory.AsyncImage(source=src, allow_stretch=True)
            carousel.add_widget(image)
        return carousel

def add_slide(dt):
    src = "http://placehold.it/480x270.png&text=slide-2&.png"
    image = Factory.AsyncImage(source=src, allow_stretch=True)
    carousel.add_widget(image)

Clock.schedule_once(add_slide,10)
carousel = Carousel(direction='right')
Example1().run()
```

issue_title: No instructions for utilizing in Arch linux (i686 / x86_64)
issue_body:
**NOTE:** The following commands assume that your user has sudo access. If not, this can be accomplished by "usermod -G wheel {user}" without quotes, as root. It is also necessary to uncomment the following line in /etc/sudoers:
%wheel ALL=(ALL) ALL


**If platform is Arch x86_64 Enable multilib repository**
=========================================
Uncomment the following in /etc/pacman.conf:
[multilib]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

**Update the package database** 
=======================
sudo pacman -Syu

**If platform is Arch x86_64:**
====================
sudo pacman -S jdk7-openjdk \
python2 \
python2-pip \
python2-kivy \
mesa-libgl \
lib32-mesa-libgl \
lib32-sdl2 \
lib32-sdl2_image \
lib32-sdl2_mixer \
sdl2_ttf \
unzip \
gcc-multilib \
gcc-libs-multilib

If prompted with the following, answer 'y' for both prompts:
::gcc-multilib and gcc are in conflict. Remove gcc?
::gcc-libs-multilib and gcc-libs are in conflict. Remove gcc-libs?

**If platform is Arch x86:**
==================
sudo pacman -S jdk7-openjdk \
python2 \
python2-pip \
python2-kivy \
mesa-libgl \
sdl2 \
sdl2_image \
sdl2_mixer \
sdl2_ttf \
unzip


**Install Cython:**
===========
sudo pip2 install cython


**Install buildozer:**
==============
git clone http://github.com/kivy/buildozer
sudo python2 setup.py install

**DONE!**

From here you should be able to run the buildozer build process.
- buildozer init
- modify the buildozer.conf as necessary
- buildozer android debug serve

issue_title: intercept term in splines
issue_body:
I'm trying to understand cubic spline generation. As far as I can see from the output of
```
x = np.linspace(0., 1., 100)
y1 = dmatrix("bs(x, df=6, degree=3, include_intercept=True)", {"x": x})
print(y1)
y2 = dmatrix("bs(x, df=6, degree=3, include_intercept=False)", {"x": x})
print(y2)
```
the `y1` (with intercept) and `y2` (without intercept) are the same. Is there something I am missing? Thanks!

issue_title: Test failures with 2.7 and 3.6
ENH: Remove duplicate code for `_sanitize_dates` and `_init_session`
issue_body:
`_sanitize_dates` and `_init_session` are both in `_utils.py` and in `base.py`

so it's simpler for people who don't want an oriented object approach for their `DataReader` but just (simple) functions.

We need to remove from `_BaseReader` such methods to avoid duplicate code.

issue_title: QHeaderView.setSectionResize on Python 3 works randomly
issue_body:
Hi qtpy,

As discussed in #65, I've got a problem, and it occurs at random.

**Reproducible**

```bash
# Ubuntu 16.04
$ apt-get update
$ apt-get install python3-pyside git
$ git clone https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy.git
$ export PYTHONPATH=$(pwd)/qtpy
$ python3
>>> from PySide import QtGui
>>> # setSectionResizeMode is added by qtpy on import
>>> assert not hasattr(QtGui.QHeaderView, "setSectionResizeMode")
>>> import sys
>>> from qtpy import QtWidgets
>>> app = QtWidgets.QApplication(sys.argv)
>>> widget = QtWidgets.QTreeView()
>>> qheaderview = widget.header()
>>> qheaderview.setSectionResizeMode(qheaderview.Fixed)
AttributeError: 'PySide.QtGui.QHeaderView' object has no attribute 'setSectionResizeMode'
```

The error there is thrown 9/10 times I try. :(

If I remove the line with `hasattr`, it works 10/10, so I figure some magic (threaded initialisation?) must be occurring on this line.

issue_title: Set host and path at build time
issue_body:
The host and path are configuration settings specific to the server. Therefore they should be customized in the swagger spec for each deployment.

This is different from reading in the template file, as a template may be used with different host and path configurations, for instance in test and production environments. A separate way of specifying the host and path, such as arguments to the command line interface is needed.

issue_title: `SnippetEvent` should be loggable
issue_body:
Right now logging event object directly does not show the content of the event, which makes debugging difficult.
`logging.info(event)` should log the full content of the event.

issue_body:
Right now we have this logic in a couple of places. We should consolidate them.
Also the current logic does not check illegal characters for test name like space, we should fix that as well.

issue_title: Potential "Control flow issues" detected by Coverity
issue_body:
Updating to [brotli 1.0.5 in Firefox](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1474122), coverity discovered two new issues (CID 1437666 & 1437667)

For 
https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/c/enc/hash_rolling_inc.h#L131
Coverity says: 

> dead_error_condition: The condition (position & 0UL) != 0UL cannot be true.

And for 
https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/c/enc/hash_rolling_inc.h#L164

> dead_error_condition: The condition (cur_ix & 0UL) != 0UL cannot be true.

As they do not seem to be false positive, I prefer to report them!

issue_title: Establish a comms channel for contributors
issue_body:
This ticket is for discussing + establishing a comms channel for contributors.

Perhaps [Gitter](https://gitter.im/google/python-fire) is the best way to do this.
@saurabhkpatel suggested Slack, but with Slack you need to specify a list of domains from which to allow email addresses, whereas I want anyone to be able to join the conversation. There are ways around this like [slackin](https://github.com/rauchg/slackin), but at a glance it looks like I'd have to commit to maintaining a server running slackin indefinitely for this to be a viable workaround, which I'm not prepared to do.

+@jtratner @nealmcb @saurabhkpatel @jgarst @keatinge as an FYI

issue_title: Inaccurate conversion from kilometers to miles
issue_body:
geopy/units.py uses the exact multiplier, 1.609344, to convert miles
into kilometers but an *approximate* multiplier, 0.621371192, to convert
kilometers into miles.  This leads to an error of about 4mm over a
quarter meridian.  Not much, you might say.  But why be so sloppy in the
first place?  Either express the multiplier with 17 significant figures
(0.62137119223733397) or divide by 1.609344.

issue_title: Can't update _selected_rows from python
issue_body:
I'm wondering how to change the selected rows from Python. I couldn't figure out a way to propagate the `._selected_rows` attribute to the browser and have it update the table styling.

I want to synchronize the index of the selected row in a qgrid table with a Holoviews Stream. Right now I have the qgrid->Stream direction working (i.e., click on a row, and it updates the stream), but I'd like the reverse as well (i.e., whenever the stream updates, I want the highlighted row to change in the browser).

On Python 3.6.5, qgrid from git master, latest jupyterlab via pip.

issue_title: timeseries.cone_rolling needs refactoring
issue_body: @justinlent and I just looked at the code in `cone_rolling`. While functional, as we're relying on that code more and more it should be more robust. There seems to be an issue with <= logics, the warmup-period is obsolete, it's not tested etc. Probably a clean rewrite is the best path forward.

issue_title: Issues with Unicode in path
issue_body:
I saw that you fixed #153 by handling Unicode characters in the querystring, but there still appear to be issues when said Unicode characters are in the URL path itself. If you pass a mock URL with a Unicode character in it to Responses and then request the same URL via Requests, you get a connection refused error.

[This is the line](https://github.com/getsentry/responses/blob/master/responses.py#L166) where it fails, when I stepped through the debugger.

I'm using Python 2.7.13 and Responses 0.8.0. Please let me know if I need to clarify this issue. Thanks!

issue_title: Resolver returns a bad url
issue_body:
I'm using Django 1.7 (I know) and I just upgraded from raven 5.24.3 to 5.32.0, which causes me some new issues with the new UrlResolver. In some case, the culprit get translated from path like `v1/cart/mine/items/` to `/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{api_name}/{resource_name}/{pk}/items` which then goes to the transaction tag in sentry.

I investigated a bit and found out the culprit is `raven.contrib.django.resolver.RouteResolver._resolve` which calls itself recursively and increase the `prefix` size for whatever reason...  
Do you have any clue how I can fix this please?

issue_title: raven.Client should support comparison
issue_body: It's useful to compare two instances (via e.g. ==), especially when we write test cases and want to assert about how a logging handler has been configured.

issue_title: Collect core dumps
issue_body:
We need to collect core dumps in case of seg fault.

That means:
  - Dockerfile-centos7.in needs to create `/tmp/cores` folder
  - Jenkinsfile needs to collect everything in `/tmp/cores`

issue_title: Macports and Anaconda package for Galsim?
issue_body:
I just found Galsim, thanks for creating it!
I'm very interested in the methods you describe in the paper and would like to try it out / do some benchmarks for the application I care about.

But don't have much time and it seems galsim installation on my Macbook is non-trivial.

Any chance you can make this work for Macports?
```
port install galsim
```
(I see there's some notes on Macports [here](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/releases/1.2/INSTALL.md#ii-mac-osx-108-and-earlier), but I'd prefer not to have to install `tmv-cpp` manually and then learn how to pass the right options to `scons`. If you contribute Portfiles for `tmv-cpp` and `galsim` and put yourself as maintainer I'm sure they will be accepted by Macports)

Alternatively, I have conda, so if you could make this work, running galsim would also be easy for me:
```
conda install galsim
```

issue_title: repr and str on transformed single summed galaxies
issue_body:
There seems to be a bug when trying to print the str or repr of an GSObject that consists of a sum of a single object. Example below. 
```
import galsim
gal1 = galsim.Sum([galsim.Transformation(galsim.Exponential(scale_radius=0.34586814810813454, flux=1049961.0753931333, gsparams=None), jac=[[0.5398181795385592, 0.17977371761576255], [0.17977371761576255, 1.9123449870247538]], offset=galsim.PositionD(x=0.0, y=0.0), flux_ratio=1.0, gsparams=None)], gsparams=None)
gal2 = gal1.dilate(1)
print str(gal1)
(galsim.Exponential(scale_radius=0.345868148108, flux=1049961.07539).shear(galsim.Shear(g1=-0.559720831877,g2=0.146624596656)))

print str(gal2)

print repr(gal1)

issue_title: HSM ShapeData
issue_body: The `hsm.ShapeData` returned by `hsm.EstimateShear()` seems to always have `corrected_g1,2 == -10.` and `corrected_shape_err == 0.`.  The `ShapeData` docs refer to a `meas_type` parameter that would switch between e1,2 and g1,2 but I don't see this parameter in the `EstimateShear` arg list or in `HSMParams`.  Is there no way to estimate g1,2 from the python layer?  Is it possible to enable the shape error calculation?

issue_title: Partition function output doesn't seem to agree with other section measurements
issue_body:
I am trying to compute the dependence between functions and I noticed that the partition function doesn't seem to be computed for all the sections:

```python
from neurom.features import get
from neurom import ezy

n = ezy.load_neuron('./test_data/swc/Neuron.swc')

get('number_of_sections', n)
# Out[10]: array([ 84.])

sl = get('section_lengths', n)
len(sl)
# Out[14]: 84

sp = get('partition', n)
len(sp)
# Out[16]: 40

```

Notice that the length of the array is not equal to the number of sections, as expected.

issue_title: FA Pro - React Native - Invariant Violation Error
issue_body:
Hey!

I'm trying to get Font Awesome 5 Pro into a new app I'm making 🎉 

I've followed the documentation as best as I can, as there's no specific docs for React Native.

In my App.js - 
```javascript
import fontawesome from '@fortawesome/fontawesome'
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome' // Maybe not needed?
import faCoffee from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-light/faCoffee'

fontawesome.config =
  { familyPrefix: 'fal'
  }
fontawesome.library.add(faCoffee)
```

In a React component's `render` - 

```jsx
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome'
<FontAwesomeIcon icon={route.icon} />
```

Where `route.icon` is currently `coffee`.

This, however, causes the following error to be thrown: 
```
Could not find icon, Object {
  "iconName": "coffee",
  "prefix": "fas",
}
- node_modules/@fortawesome/react-fontawesome/index.js:450:37 in log
```

Which is interesting, because according to the docs this is how I should be able to change the default prefix.

If I explicitly ask for that prefix using

```jsx
import FontAwesomeIcon from '@fortawesome/react-fontawesome'
<FontAwesomeIcon icon={[ 'fal', route.icon ]} />
```

This causes a _different_ error to be thrown:
```
Invariant Violation: View config not found for name path

This error is located at:
    in path (created by FontAwesomeIcon$1)
    in svg (created by FontAwesomeIcon$1)
    in FontAwesomeIcon$1 (at grid2.js:58)
    in RCTText (at Text.js:557)
    in Text (at rkText.js:82)
    in RkText (at grid2.js:55)
    in RCTView (at View.js:112)
```

This happens if I remove the config change as well, and also if I change the icon import from the `light` version to the `solid` one.

Any ideas?

issue_title: Inventory in workflow schema uses unique ID
issue_body:
The issue I'm running into is that the schema's for workflows use a unique ID for inventories (not for job_templates). This creates a problem, since the ID is unique and created when the inventory is created. So, if you intend to automatically create an inventory and then use it in a workflow, you don't know what the inventory ID is easily.

Here is an example of a schema:

- job_template: aws-infrastructure
  success_nodes:
  - inventory_source: aws (aws-inventory - 59)
    success_nodes:
    - job_template: aws-openshift-install

It would be so much easier if you could refer to an inventory by it's name and not have the " -59" in there.

Here is a bunch of background that led to me realizing the issue: https://github.com/strategicdesignteam/openshift-ansible-contrib/tree/master/reference-architecture/ansible-tower-integration

issue_title: HA test questions
issue_body:
Hi, 

I've been running some HA testing with Patroni installed from the RAFT branch (happy to re-test with etcd if needed). 

I'm confused by the following flows:

1. Start three nodes, wait for leader and standbys to boostrap
1. Stop all nodes
1. Start the master node
1. Start the standby nodes
1. Seems like there is a timeline bump every time 

And also:

1. Start three nodes, wait for leader and standbys to boostrap
1. Stop a single standby 
1. Stop the master
1. As expected, no promotion as the standby can't get consensus to take leader
1. Start the master
1. Seems like there is a promotion on the standby and a timeline bump every time 

It seems like the correct behavior here would be to stay on the old master and hold timeline if at all possible.

Cheers,
James

issue_title: Header Validation not being performed as expected
issue_body:
### Description

OpenAPI/Swagger spec defines a set of header(s) in its response block, like such:

    responses:
        '200':
          description: My Description.
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/MyObject'
          headers:
            Link:
              type: string

Appending the header is relatively easy using Connexion:

    return 'Foo', 200, {'Link': 'FOO'}

Which is returned correctly, however when the header is not added, Connexion is not marking it as a warning or throwing any errors for not matching the spec.

### Expected behavior

5XX response or something similar to indicate an internal error due to the API not satisfying the spec.

### Actual behavior

200 OK status code, response is returned anyway without the defined header.

### Additional Info

I checked in the repo itself, and can confirm that there is a code block dedicated to validating headers to make sure they match the spec:

`connexion.decorators.response.py`


    if response_definition and response_definition.get("headers"):
        # converting to set is needed to support python 2.7
        response_definition_header_keys = set(response_definition.get("headers").keys())
        header_keys = set(headers.keys())
        missing_keys = response_definition_header_keys - header_keys
        if missing_keys:
            pretty_list = ', '.join(missing_keys)
            msg = ("Keys in header don't match response specification. "
                   "Difference: {0}").format(pretty_list)
            raise NonConformingResponseHeaders(message=msg)


May I be missing something in particular in this case? Maybe not related to Connexion? 

PS - Running latest version of Connexion

issue_title: [gtk3] Prefs GtkFrame border
issue_body:
In the gtk2 version, GtkFrames (there's plenty of them in Preferences) had their title displayed only and the frame itself hidden (GTK_SHADOW_NONE).

In the gtk3 version, the frame itself is shown (probably GTK_SHADOW_ETCHED_IN) resulting in a much more crowded look.

Unless this change was intentional, I'd consider reverting to the previous look.

issue_title: iRODSUser.dn() assumes single user-dn mapping
issue_body:
In case the user has more than one authentication name (which is completelly valid) iRODSUser.dn() throws an exception.
e.g.
```
>>> user = sess.users.get("jankowsk")
>>> print user.dn                      
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/irods/user.py", line 21, in dn
    return query.one()[UserAuth.user_dn]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/irods/query.py", line 227, in one
    raise MultipleResultsFound()
irods.exception.MultipleResultsFound
```

Instead query.one() query.all() may be used:
```
>>> query = sess.query(UserAuth.user_dn).filter(User.name == "jankowsk")
>>> [ i[UserAuth.user_dn] for i in query.all()]
['/C=PL/O=GRID/O=PSNC/CN=Michal Jankowski', '/C=PL/O=PCSS/OU=KDM/CN=Michal Jankowski']
```

issue_title: asizeof does not properly size numpy arrays
issue_body:
Although numpy is not a part of the python standard library, it is a part of many python developer's standard toolkits. Therefore it seems like it would be useful to properly size numpy arrays. Currently asizeof does not properly size numpy arrays. eg:

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from pympler import asizeof
>>> example = np.arange(1000)
>>> asizeof.asizeof(example)
80
```
Clearly, an array of 1000 values should take more than 80 bytes of memory.
Fortunately numpy arrays have an "nbytes" property that indicates their size, thus

```
>>> example.nbytes
8000
```
It may be possible to get an accurate idea of numpy array memory usage using this property.

I ran these commands in Python 3.4 with pympler 0.4.3

issue_title: pubsub can't infer a connection when running locally.
issue_body:
When running locally with ``gcloud auth`` providing application default credentials, pubsub is unable to infer a connection, so this will fail:

```python
import pubsub
pubsub.set_default_project('my-project-id')
topic = pubsub.Topic('my-topic')
topic.publish(...)
```

with ``EnvironmentError: Connection could not be inferred.``

The fix is to set an explicit connection using adc:
```python
credentials = GoogleCredentials.get_application_default()
pubsub.set_default_connection(pubsub.Connection(credentials))
```

However, storage and datastore do not have this issue. It seems unnecessarily to do this just for pubsub.

issue_body:
This issue is re-posted from an internal ticket. (Googlers: b/63330731)

--------------------------------

Using the google-cloud-python BigQuery client, loading data from GCS fails repeatably on first load due to schema issues, then succeeds on retry.  Using _any_ other method of BQ load from GCS, this behavior is not observed.  Occurs on version 0.24 and 0.25 of the BigQuery client.

As a workaround, the customer reimplemented the BQ load from GCS process using JSON api, which also works correctly.

issue_title: The read_rows method in the bigtable client does not provide an interface to specify open/closed key ranges
issue_body:
The read_rows method of the Table class in the bigtable client is designed such that the results are inclusive of the start key but exclusive of the end key. That seems a reasonable choice, until one considers that keys may be designed to accumulate in reverse order (as recommended in the docs: https://cloud.google.com/bigtable/docs/schema-design-time-series). In that case, it is necessary to flip the natural order of the keys to process a query -i.e. if I query from time t0 to t1, I will create two corresponding keys k0 and k1, but k1 will be the start key and k0 the end key in the call to read_rows.

Currently, the specification of open and closed for the start and end key is hard-coded in the _create_row_request method, and that makes it difficult for an end user of the library to alter the default behavior. Consider adding an option to the read_rows method, or better yet, an attribute of a Table instance, such that queries can implement different behaviors with respect to the start and end keys.

in `train.py`
```python
import argparse                     
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np
                          
tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.INFO)

def input_fn():
    features = np.random.random((10000, 10))
    labels = np.random.random((10000, 5))


    features_dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_generator(
        lambda: iter(features), tf.float32, (tf.TensorShape([10])))
    labels_dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_generator(
        lambda: iter(labels), tf.float32, (tf.TensorShape([5])))
    dataset = tf.data.Dataset.zip((features_dataset, labels_dataset))
    dataset = dataset.batch(32)
    iterator = dataset.make_one_shot_iterator()
    return iterator.get_next()

def run_experiment(job_dir):
    train_dataset = lambda: input_fn()
    eval_dataset = lambda:  input_fn()

    train_spec = tf.estimator.TrainSpec(train_dataset, max_steps=1000)
    eval_spec = tf.estimator.EvalSpec(eval_dataset, steps=16)

    run_config = tf.estimator.RunConfig(
        model_dir=job_dir,
        save_summary_steps=1,
        log_step_count_steps=2,
    )

    def model_fn(features, labels, mode):
        out = tf.contrib.layers.fully_connected(tf.cast(features, tf.float32),5)
        loss = tf.contrib.losses.mean_squared_error(out, tf.cast(labels, tf.float32))
        train_op = tf.train.AdamOptimizer().minimize(
            loss,
            global_step=tf.train.get_or_create_global_step())
        return tf.estimator.EstimatorSpec(
            mode=mode,
            predictions=out,
            loss=loss,
            train_op=train_op,
        )

    estimator = tf.estimator.Estimator(model_fn, config=run_config)
    tf.estimator.train_and_evaluate(estimator, train_spec, eval_spec)


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument(
        '--job-dir',
        type=str,
        help='GCS or local paths to save run files and output',
        required=True,
    )
    return parser.parse_known_args()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    args, _ = parse_args()
    run_experiment(args.job_dir)
```

**Instructions:**

from folder root run
```sh
PYTHONPATH=$PWD gcloud ml-engine local train \
    --module-name training.train \
    --package-path training/ \
    --job-dir job_dir
```

JOB_NAME="myjob"; gcloud ml-engine jobs submit training $JOB_NAME \

--config config.yaml

Note, the iteration number 313 is a consistent stopping point with this example. Remote training regiment appears to be ignoring the `max_steps=1000` argument and uses some other criteria.

issue_body:
This is a distillation of this report from **many** other issues. Large thanks to @anorth2 and @dmontag for reporting and helping refine the issue.

Issues that have been partially resolved (except for the CPU spike) are being collapsed into this one:

- #4274 
- #4563 
- #3965
- (more to come?)

----

**Core issue**: there is a [spinlock bug][1] in gRPC (present in all recent versions `1.6.x`, `1.7.x`, `1.8.1`). A "bandaid" [fix][2] exists but has not been merged (as of noon Pacific on December 15, 2017).

**Update**: The fix was rolled back, but will be rolled forward (https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/13918).

**Potential workaround**: compile `grpcio` from source ([e.g.][3]) while including the bandaid [fix][2] or just use the 64-bit `manylinux` [wheel][4] that I already created (I may be open to creating Mac OS X wheels, not sure about Windows)

[1]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/9688
[2]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/13665
[3]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/4274#issuecomment-351863030
[4]: https://github.com/dhermes/google-cloud-pubsub-performance/blob/master/grpcio-1.7.4.dev1-cp36-cp36m-manylinux1_x86_64.whl

issue_title: serializing a numpy array instance referenced in some other data structure causes serialization of the latter to fail
issue_body:
Using dill 0.2.2 with numpy 1.9.2 and Python 2.7.9, the following runs without any problems:

```
import dill
import numpy
x = numpy.ones((2,2))
data = {}
data['x'] = x
dill.dumps(data)
```

However, if I try serializing `x` immediately before `data`, Python raises an AssertionError when attempting the second serialization:

```
import dill
import numpy
x = numpy.ones((2,2))
data = {}
data['x'] = x
dill.dumps(x)
dill.dumps(data)
```

The same problem occurs if `data` is a list and I append `x` to it.

The problem only seems to occur when `x` is a numpy array; I wasn't able to reproduce the error when I set `x` to a variety of other data types (e.g., lists, dicts, Pandas DataFrame instances, ...). Any idea why this is occurring?

issue_title: Fix SetupPy to use global knowledge of the BUILD graph to consistently minify deps
issue_body:
Right now SetupPy just looks in the active target closure when calculating which targets and 3rdparty deps an exportable target should include in its distribution.  This falls short whenever the exportable target depends on a non-exportable target whose exportable owner is not in the current closure.  A good example of this is an auxialiary pantsbuild sdist (contrib) that depends on specific thin pants targets - say base.  The closure for this sort of exportable contrib will _not_ contain the primary pants sdist target which would include everything and be too fat a dep.  As such, the current algorithm has no way to know the base target should be elided from the contrib sdist sources and instead be dependended upon via a pantsbuild.pants setup_requires.  Fix this state of affairs.

This fix will form the last piece of solving https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues/1126

issue_title: Deprecate bundle options on CLI, move to BUILD file
issue_body:
We should deprecate the CLI versions of bundle arguments, and move them into jvm_app target declarations in BUILD files (Options registered in BundleCreate class should be moved to the payload in JvmApp). This would allow us to get closer to being able to reproduce deployments.

The common case should be that a deploy job doesn't need to pass any arguments to ./pants bundle, because they're already specified on the target.

issue_title: Support composing named "bundle"/"sourceset" targets into a JvmApp
issue_body: There are workarounds (symlinks) in place to support JvmApps consuming bundles/sources that exist outside of their root directory. Instead, we should support composition of named targets.

issue_title: Improve C++ external library compiling + linking
issue_body:
The current iteration of the ctypes-based native backend compiles and links all `native_external_library` targets in the target closure, and does not partition compiles or links based on which external library targets belong to which dependee targets. 

The scope of this ticket is to rework the product produced by the `native_external_library_fetch` task to map targets to subproducts, and ensure that proper partitioning is occurring during the compile and link tasks.

issue_title: Enable tool caching by default
issue_body: Caching of shaded tools is critical for performance, but is not enabled by default. This relates to #1640 in that once that issue is fixed, we might consider enabling caching for all tasks, rather than just tools.

issue_title: replace BuildGraph's travesal argument, leveled_predicate with dep_predicate
issue_body:
Currently `transitive_subgraph_of_addresses_bfs` and `walk_transitive_dependency_graph` support a kwarg called `leveled_predicate`, which adds a condition for deciding whether to include dependencies of a target in the traversal. It takes the target that is the dependency and the depth from the root of the traversal as its parameters.

One issue with this is that it makes it difficult to implement predicates dependent upon attributes of the relationship between the target and its dependency. For instance, whether the dependency is exported or not.

I propose replacing that predicate with a predicate that receives the current target and the dependency. This way we can use attributes of either target to determine whether to include the dependency.


Concern: How can you still support intransitive if you don't know what depth away from the root target you are at?

 I can reimplement intransitive support by keeping a lookup table of the root targets and checking the current target to see if it is a root or not when evaluating whether to keep an intransitive target. The one issue with this is that it can change the traversal order when there are multiple roots.

issue_title: Support batch validation of CSV rows in `clldutils.csvw`
issue_body: Rather than raising `ValueError` at the first data error when reading CSV files, logging (and skipping invalid rows) should be used to allow batch validation of complete datasets.

Example:

```yaml
  Function:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function

      FunctionName: !Ref FunctionName

issue_title: Consider replacing gevent with eventlet
issue_body:
As reported in #176, gevent does not work with Python 2.7.9 (now the default shipped with Ubuntu Vivid) and appears to be unmaintained. The last release of gevent was 2014-04-30 and the last commit to the repo was  2014-12-13. The [relevant bug](https://github.com/gevent/gevent/issues/477) was filed on 2014-09-12 and still hasn't been addressed.

By contrast, eventlet fixed this issue in 0.16, released on 2014-12-30 and continues to be in active development.

If you think this is a viable option I'd be happy to try to create a PR for it. The two libs are fairly similar so I'm hoping it won't be too much work.

issue_title: broken auth on payloads containing tilde chars
issue_body:
client.py uses urllib.urlencode, which percent-encodes the tilde character (~) as %7E in order to generate the signature. This conflicts with RFC 5849, section 3.6, which states that ~ must not be percent-encoded.

On the other hand, the base_url is encoded via requests in which [the tilde issue](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/issues/369#issuecomment-3953802) has [been corrected](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/pull/426/files#diff-5956087d5835a57d9ef6fff974f6fd9bR399) and no longer matches urllib's weird behavior.

Ultimately this results in a 403 error (client id omitted below):
```
"Unable to authenticate the request. Provided 'signature' is not valid for the provided client ID, or the provided 'client' is not valid.
The signature was checked against the URL: /maps/api/geocode/json?address=560+Maple+Street+South+%2310~&components=administrative_area%3AMN%7Clocality%3AWaconia%7Cpostal_code%3A55387&client=
If this does not match the URL you requested, please ensure that your request is URL encoded correctly. Learn more: https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/business/webservices/auth"
```

Although tildes should be rare, ideal behavior would probably be either:

1. Use the same encoding library for the base_url and for computing the authed_url, or
2. Raise an informative exception on disallowed characters (incl. tilde)

instead of waiting for a non-specific 403.

pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20)

Perhaps `Client` isn't intended to be thread-safe, but that should at least be documented somewhere.

Thanks in advance... :+1:

issue_title: churches recognized as person instead of corporation
issue_body:
I extracted [8964 entities from Wikidata related to the Wikipedia category Churches in the United States](https://github.com/az0/entity-metadata/blob/master/data/wikidata-church.csv), and then I manually filtered the list to 7000 records that really look like churches. I pulled a simple random sample of 100 records, and I fed them into the probablepeople bulk parsing service.

The following 7 records were incorrectly recognized as people instead of churches. 

St. Augustine Church
Bethany Lutheran Church
Augustus Lutheran Church
Grace Episcopal Church
St. Joseph Church
Grace Church

Would you like a PR with this as training data? Is 7 items of training data enough? 

Because this was a simple random sample, we can estimate the error interval at 3% to 14%, so on the population of 7000 records that is 217 to 1006 errors.

By the way, this can be complicated because [Church is surname](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Church_(surname)). Looking at the list above, it gets complicated because Bethany or Grace can be either a given name or partial church name.

issue_title: Tox should set usedevelop=false
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

All too often, we are seeing differences between our test environment and the real installed environment.

Two factors:

 * [ ] Use `src/` source structure to avoid `kolibri/` folder being in the immediate path when running for instance test commands. #1953 already addresses this
 * [ ] Tox should build a real `.whl`, then install it, and then run the tests. This issue addresses this specific need.

Underlying problem is that when we run tests, our development environment is different because it may have a different path access the host system, but also that `kolibri/dist` may or may not be populated. And the development environment may also have a different set of requirements installed in its virtualenv.

Solution:

1. Set `usedevelop=false` in tox.ini
1. Build a `.whl` output in the first stages of Travis and reuse across all test envs. See also #2975 
1. Remove the need to test the build since it will always be built (aka. conditional tests)

### Expected behavior

That we can reproduce the problem reported in #3308 in a Travis VM.. and then fix it!

issue_title: Move to py.test and take advantage of it
issue_body:
We want to move to py.test so that:
- we can select to only run parser tests with one tree builder (and tree walker tests with only one tree walker, etc.)
- we can make use of the plugin API to create a means of dumping test failures and build support for expected failures into the testsuites from html5lib-tests
- it is easier to create nicer error messages
- better support for skipped and expected failures so we can walk towards having a constant number of tests across all platforms, optionally skipped

https://github.com/gsnedders/html5lib-python/tree/expected_failures_pytest is my current WIP branch implementing this.
## Status:
### Needed to fix failures:
- [x] Make sure tests from generators run in a deterministic order
- [x] Use pytest-expect
### The rest:
- [ ] Move tokenizer and serialiser tests over to generating test objects from the JSON files.
- [ ] Refactor support.py.
- [x] Check the submodule is imported (see #122).

issue_body:

issue_title: Wrong instantiation of urllib2.HTTPSHandler
issue_body:
Here https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/commit/5820c980f4733606c93149e62e7a6737785eda6d#diff-16683aec6de1b227110863506f6fb267R380

`handlers.append(urllib2.HTTPSHandler(self._ssl_context))` is wrong because HTTPSHandler has `debuglevel` as first parameter

````
 class HTTPSHandler(AbstractHTTPHandler):

        def __init__(self, debuglevel=0, context=None):
            AbstractHTTPHandler.__init__(self, debuglevel)
            self._context = context
````

please correct line 380 to `handlers.append(urllib2.HTTPSHandler(context=self._ssl_context))`

Isn't this a security issue too?

issue_title: `charm build` should clean up removed managed files
issue_body:
`charm build` should compare the old and new `.build.manifest` files and clean up any files that are signed in the old manifest but not present in the new manifest.  Not doing this is causing the following issues:

* https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/issues/11
* https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/issues/19
* https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/issues/33

It is also causing new wheelhouse deps to be added alongside of, instead of replacing, old versions of the deps, which causes issues during deployment.

Fixed version requirements cause problems
issue_body:
I have a job that requires Pillow version >= 3.4, but girder_worker has Pillow 3.2.0 as a hard requirement.  The core plugins will NOT load with a different version of Pillow.  Requirements should allow a version range or only specify a minimum version unless there is a known reason to have a fixed version.

Since requirements.txt should be the frozen version used for testing, setup.py should either specify packages explicitly (rather than reading a file), or, when reading the requirements.txt file should change simple == version requirements to ~=.

issue_title: healthcheck: next-hop argument should not be sent through ip_address()
issue_body:
healthcheck.py's `--next-hop` argument is specified with `type=ip_address`. However, ip_address() really constructs an IP network, so whatever's passed in will come out as a /32 (in the IPv4 case), which results in this API message (note the /32 on the next-hop):

```
DEBUG[healthcheck] exabgp: announce route 10.20.9.99/32 next-hop 10.20.13.48/32 med 100
announce route 10.20.9.99/32 next-hop 10.20.13.48/32 med 100
```

If I'm following the flow right, the main process then rejects this announce:

```
02:23:18 | 32     | reactor       | async | testing | problem with function
02:23:18 | 32     | reactor       | async | testing | illegal IP address string passed to inet_pton`
```

Using the same constructor for both the `--ip` and `--next-hop` arguments seems like the wrong thing to do here.

issue_body:

##### OS

```

##### ENVIRONMENT 
```
# ./sbin/exabgp --di
#
```

<!--- You can also use gist.github.com links for larger files -->

##### CONFIGURATION
```
neighbor 192.168.0.110 {
                router-id 192.168.0.120;
                local-address 192.168.0.120;
		description "internet-router-1a";
                local-as 10;
		peer-as 10;
		family vpn-flowspec {
					ipv4 flow-vpn;
					ipv6 flow-vpn;
					}
 		flow {
		route drop-synfloods-ipv4 {
				rd 10:10;
				match {
					protocol tcp;
					tcp-flags syn;
					}
				then {
					discard;
					extended-community target:10:10 ;
					}
				}

		route drop-synfloods-ipv6 {
				rd 10:10;
				match {
					next-header tcp;
                                        # protocol 41;
					tcp-flags syn;
					}

					extended-community target:10:10 ;
					}
				}

issue_title: docker-py build cache differs from docker build cache
issue_body:
I build images in 2 ways:
- docker build .
- with docker-py, e.g.

``` python
import docker
client = docker.Client()
for line in client.build(path="."):
    print line
```

The caches for these two methods seem to be independent.  If I change an ADDed file, for example, and then rebuild the image using 'docker build', a subsequent build using docker-py starts from the same ADD command, rather than noticing the image is up to date.  And vice versa.

docker-py 0.6.0
docker 1.3.0

I recognize this could be a bug with docker.  If that's true, please let me know and I'll move this.

issue_title: Add utility to create a build context from a git repository.
issue_body:
The reason is because it won't work if you pass a private git repository as the build URL.

See docker/compose#2856 for more information, including a simple test-case by @zkf.

Instead, `docker-py` should follow the same steps as `docker`: if the build URL is a git repository, it should clone the repo locally and pass as a tarball to the daemon.

I believe the problem is caused by how `docker-py` performs a build ([here](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/api/build.py#L15)) instead of following the same steps as `docker`.

Please let me know if I'm misunderstanding the problem or if there's any other way I can help.

issue_title: Handling docker permissions: using a subprocess
issue_body:
So currently the user running this library has to have or root permissions or be in the docker group (which is equivalent to root). For daily development use (I use this library to prepare environments for unit testing) both of these approaches seems dangerous to do on a regular basis.

I would suggest that this library spawns a subprocess which then asks for escaled privileges to root. And then library communicates with that process only. At the time of escalation user could be asked for the root or sudo password. This would allow user to control much better when a regular user (and this library) can access Docker.

A similar issue happens if one is using virtualenv. If you just develop using virtualenv as a regular user and then want to run a script which has to access Docker, but you do not want to have this regular user always permissions of docker group, you can use sudo. But this then breaks out of the virtualenv when executed.

issue_title: skip_initialization and initial_chains can leave embeddings in a weird state
issue_body: the chain datastructure is generally expected to be an in-tree.  when using initialization parameters, it's possible to escape the initialization phase where chains are represented as a "forest" of points, each its own parent

issue_title: Add configurable timeout to all API requests
No need of using defaultdict for implementing get_cardinality method of various models.
issue_body:
I think a normal dict would also suffice this implementation.Its because, defaultdict is similar to the usual dictionary (dict) container, but it has one difference: that it is initialized with a function that takes no arguments and provides the default value for a nonexistent key. Thus , a defaultdict will never raise a KeyError if you try to get an item with a key that is not currently in the dictionary unlike usual dict container.
Now ,here in this method we are always appending variables and its corresponding cardinality into the dict container.We are never retrieving a value using a key(either an existing or non-existing key). So ,if their is no retrieval then a normal dict would itself suffice .

I think one thing is lacking in this implementation . If node passed while calling get_cardinality, is not present in the model, then it should throw an error. Right now this is not happening.

###  My environment
* version of pgmpy = "0.1.6"
* Operating System = "ubuntu 16.04"

### Steps to reproduce
import numpy as np
from pgmpy.models import MarkovModel
from pgmpy.factors.discrete import DiscreteFactor

student = MarkovModel([('Alice', 'Bob'), ('Bob', 'Charles')])
phi1 = DiscreteFactor(['Alice', 'Bob'], cardinality=[2, 3], values=np.random.rand(6))
phi2 = DiscreteFactor(['Bob', 'Charles'], cardinality=[3, 3], values=np.random.rand(9))
student.add_factors(phi1, phi2)
student.get_cardinality('Alice')
student.get_cardinality('Bob')
student.get_cardinality('Dexter')

### Expected behaviour
>>> 2
>>> 3
>>> ValueError: Node not present in the Graph

### Actual behaviour
>>> 2
>>> 3
>>> None

issue_title: Explicitly ask for generator
issue_body:
When iterating over paginated data, only return generator if explicitly requested.   
One possible way of doing this is:
``` python
for item in resp().pages():
    print(item)
``` 
By doing this, it's also possible to  pass extra params like specify the max number of pages that should be fetched, or the maxi number of results that should be yielded:
``` python
for item in resp().pages(max_pages=2, max_items=40):
    print(item)

# in this example, the for loop will stop after two pages are fetched or 40 items are yielded, 
# witch ever comes first.
```

This is one idea, other suggestions are welcome.

issue_title: SIGKILL can't be called by signal() on Windows
issue_body:
> On Windows, signal() can only be called with SIGABRT, SIGFPE, SIGILL, SIGINT, SIGSEGV, or SIGTERM. A ValueError will be raised in any other case.

https://docs.python.org/2/library/signal.html#signal.signal (python 2)
https://docs.python.org/3/library/signal.html#signal.signal (python 3)

The following occurrences of `SIGKILL` are therefore breaking service-manager on Windows...

https://github.com/hmrc/service-manager/blob/master/servicemanager/smprocess.py#L16
and
https://github.com/hmrc/service-manager/blob/master/servicemanager/subprocess.py#L1555

issue_title: Rename to "pipelines"
issue_body:
# Context

Data Packages, and Frictionless Data specifications, are essentially part of the protocol and inner workings of this package, but the package itself does not really require much knowledge about these specs. Branding it as "Data Packages" is misleading, and potentially would confuse users into thinking that a knowledge of data packages is required to use this package.

I think we should rename the package to simply `pipelines` or `pipeline`.

What are your thoughts @akariv 

Any opinion on this @brew @roll @danfowler @vitorbaptista @amercader @rufuspollock ?

issue_title: USAFCCA precedential status is often wrong
issue_body:
Looks like the site may have changed since the scraper was last updated. Among other things, the site gives several statuses: `Merits`, `Unpublished`, `Article 73`, and `Published`.

The [scraper code](https://github.com/freelawproject/juriscraper/blob/5000a47c73877a01381ac3555518b2701797646f/juriscraper/opinions/united_states/federal_special/afcca.py#L48) treats everything as published unless the site says `Unpublished`. However, when I looked at a few opinions, I noticed that opinions with `Merits` status actually said they were not published decisions. Here's [one example of that](https://www.courtlistener.com/opinion/4434716/united-states-v-pringle/?q=&type=o&order_by=dateFiled+desc&stat_Non-Precedential=on&court=afcca):

> This is an unpublished opinion and, as such, does not serve as
> precedent under AFCCA Rule of Practice and Procedure 18.4.

So, I *think* the fix is to invert how the scraper handles things to this:

```python
            statuses.append('Published' if 'published' in text else 'Unpublished')
```

I would test it myself, but I wasn't able to get the scraper working on my machine yet.

issue_body:
Importing the `authorizenet` library fails with pip 9.0.2:

```
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/authorizenet/apicontrollers.py", line 9, in <module>
    from authorizenet import apicontrollersbase    
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/authorizenet/apicontrollersbase.py", line 11, in <module>
    from pip._vendor import requests
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/__init__.py", line 45, in <module>
    from pip.vcs import git, mercurial, subversion, bazaar  # noqa
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/vcs/mercurial.py", line 9, in <module>
    from pip.download import path_to_url
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/download.py", line 40, in <module>
    from pip._vendor import requests, six
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/__init__.py", line 98, in <module>
    from . import packages
  File "/srv/wing/.env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/packages.py", line 12, in <module>
    sys.modules['pip._vendor.requests.packages.' + mod] = sys.modules["pip._vendor." + mod]
KeyError: 'pip._vendor.urllib3.contrib'
```

Pip 9.0.2 was literally released a couple hours ago as I write this, and it seems like pip changed its vendored packages around, including upgrading `requests` and explicitly vendoring `urllib3` ([see this commit](https://github.com/pypa/pip/pull/5072/commits/2e142d3face49f776aa735a701b9f6f7208f6482)).

However, this isn't a pip problem, this is a problem with using pip's vendored version of `requests` instead of an explicit dependency.
